title.en: Torah Reading in the Synagogue: What's Happening and Why
title.zh: 会堂中的托拉诵读：正在发生什么以及为什么
title.he: קריאת התורה בבית הכנסת: מה קורה ולמה

description.en: A guide for newcomers to understand the Torah service — aliyot, blessings, the haftarah, and proper etiquette.
description.zh: 帮助新来者理解托拉仪式的指南——阿利亚、祝福、哈夫塔拉和合适的礼仪。
description.he: מדריך לחדשים להבין את תפילת קריאת התורה — עליות, ברכות, הפטרה ונימוסים נאותים.

author: Torah Light Editorial Team

chinese_historical_context.tang_dynasty: During the Tang Dynasty period in China (618-907 CE), the Torah reading tradition was already ancient, having been established over a millennium earlier.
chinese_historical_context.song_dynasty: By the Song Dynasty (960-1279), when the Kaifeng Jewish community was flourishing, this weekly Torah reading practice connected Chinese Jews to the global Jewish people.
chinese_historical_context.ming_dynasty: Throughout the Ming Dynasty (1368-1644), Jewish communities worldwide maintained this unbroken tradition of public Torah reading.
chinese_historical_context.qing_dynasty: During the Qing Dynasty (1644-1912), as China encountered more Western influence, the ancient Jewish practice of Torah reading remained constant across cultures.

dall_e_header_image.prompt: A detailed oil painting of a traditional synagogue Torah reading scene. The Torah scroll is open on the bimah (reading desk) with ornate silver decorations. A man wearing a tallit is chanting from the scroll while another holds a silver yad (Torah pointer). The congregation stands attentively. Warm golden lighting illuminates the scene. Include Hebrew text visible on the scroll and traditional Ashkenazi synagogue architecture with detailed woodwork.
dall_e_header_image.style: Classical oil painting with warm golden lighting
